pi_items.source: Verified by listing the SDS archive at /home/ga/seiscomp/var/lib/archive/2024/GE/. Only BKB, GSI, and SANI directories exist with BHZ.D waveform files. TOLI and KWP have inventory entries but NO waveform data in the archive.

Verified by running scautopick --playback in the live environment. The binary exists at /home/ga/seiscomp/bin/scautopick and successfully generates P-phase picks when bindings are configured. The setup_task.sh and verifier.py both explicitly check for scautopick bindings.

Verified by inspecting the miniSEED file headers in the SDS archive. All three waveform files (GE.BKB..BHZ.D.2024.001, GE.GSI..BHZ.D.2024.001, GE.SANI..BHZ.D.2024.001) contain data for year=2024, day-of-year=001 (January 1, 2024), with start times around 07:09:40-07:09:54 UTC.

Verified by querying the SeisComP database: SELECT latitude_value FROM Origin. The USGS-imported manual origin has latitude_value=37.4874. The metadata value 37.5 is a rounded approximation (within 0.013 degrees). Cross-referenced with USGS event us6000m0xl.

Verified by querying the SeisComP database: SELECT longitude_value FROM Origin. The USGS-imported manual origin has longitude_value=137.271. The metadata value 137.2 is a rounded approximation (within 0.071 degrees). Cross-referenced with USGS event us6000m0xl.

This is a design parameter for the verifier, not a data claim. The verifier stub does not currently use this value. Given the poor station geometry (3 stations in Indonesia spanning ~29 degrees of longitude, all at teleseismic distance ~4500-5700 km), a 15-degree tolerance is a reasonable design choice for automatically located origins.

Verified consistent across task.json description, setup_task.sh (which deletes it), export_result.sh (which checks existence, validity, mtime), and verifier.py (which scores it). Path is writable by user ga in the live environment.

Verified consistent across task.json description, setup_task.sh (which deletes it), export_result.sh (which validates JSON structure and extracts fields), and verifier.py (which scores field presence). Path is writable by user ga in the live environment.

Verified by checking binary existence in the live environment: all four tools exist at /home/ga/seiscomp/bin/ and are executable. seiscomp (main control), scautopick (phase picker), scautoloc (automatic locator), scdb (database import tool) are all part of SeisComP 7.1.2.
Verified via O*NET database. SOC code 19-2042.00 corresponds to 'Geoscientists, Except Hydrologists and Geographers', which covers seismologists who configure earthquake monitoring systems.

Verified by reading the task description and all task scripts. The task requires commissioning (setting up scautopick bindings and config) and validation (writing a Python script that queries the DB and produces a verification report).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real GEOFON network seismic data from the 2024 Noto Peninsula M7.5 earthquake (USGS id us6000m0xl, origin time 2024-01-01 07:10:09 UTC, lat 37.4874, lon 137.271, depth 10 km, magnitude 7.5 Mww). Only 3 of 5 GE-network stations have waveform data in the SDS archive: GE.BKB (Balikpapan, lat -1.1073, lon 116.9048, distance 4776 km), GE.GSI (Gunungsitoli, lat 1.3039, lon 97.5755, distance 5713 km), and GE.SANI (Sanana, lat -2.0496, lon 125.9881, distance 4548 km). Stations GE.TOLI and GE.KWP have inventory entries but NO waveform files. All waveform data is BHZ channel only at 20 sps, covering a window starting around 07:09:40-07:09:54 UTC on 2024-01-01. The SDS archive path is /home/ga/seiscomp/var/lib/archive/ with files named GE.{STA}..BHZ.D.2024.001. Station bindings go in key files at /home/ga/seiscomp/etc/key/station_GE_{STA} as lines containing 'scautopick'. When scautopick is properly configured with bindings and a Butterworth filter (e.g. BW(3,0.3,3)), playback of the archived data produces 10 automatic P-phase picks: 3 on BKB (07:11:27, 07:14:04, 07:18:08), 4 on GSI (07:10:39, 07:15:09, 07:19:12, 07:27:12), and 3 on SANI (07:10:59, 07:13:44, 07:17:47). The database connection is mysql://sysop:sysop@localhost/seiscomp. The validation script must be saved to /home/ga/validate_pipeline.py and the JSON report to /home/ga/pipeline_report.json with required fields: pick_count, origin_count, origin_lat, origin_lon, origin_depth_km, origin_time, and a station_picks array with code, pick_time, and distance_km per entry.
